name: Pati-Salam unification
summary: Partial unification: leptons are treated as the fourth color of SU(4) (Pati-Salam), and the SM SU(2)_L is mirrored by SU(2)_R that gauges right-handed isospin. Includes B-L as a gauge symmetry; right-handed neutrinos and parity restoration emerge automatically. Often a stepping stone within SO(10) breaking chains.

addresses_puzzles.note: Right-handed neutrinos required by SU(2)_R structure; B-L breaking at ~10^11 GeV sets seesaw scale.
Restored parity symmetry above the breaking scale forces theta = 0; theta is regenerated only after parity breaking and remains naturally small (Babu-Mohapatra-style).

touches_benchmarks.note: Leptoquark gauge bosons of SU(4) mediate K -> mu e at one-loop; bound on M_LQ > ~10^6 GeV from K_L -> mu e.
Right-handed currents enhance 0nubb relative to canonical seesaw.

new_fields: SU(4) x SU(2)_R gauge bosons (W_R, leptoquarks)
introduces.note: Gauge breaking scales + Higgs sector parameters.

history: Pati-Salam (1974). Independently relevant as the L-R symmetric extension of the SM; embedded in SO(10) -> Pati-Salam -> SM breaking chains. Modern phenomenology focuses on TeV-scale W_R searches at LHC (CMS, ATLAS).

references.citation: Pati and Salam, 'Lepton number as the fourth color', Phys. Rev. D 10, 275 (1974)
